mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and subdued consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 2 percentage points, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of cases following two consecutive months of falling HOUST values.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment surpassing 130 million indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when PAYEMS crosses the 130 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces tighter financial conditions and higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial production and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting equity valuations and yielding positive forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined supply signal falls below 0.01 over a rolling 3-year window, or 20-day returns are negative when both series expand.
